core_mechanism: a sharp relaxation-to-equilibrium threshold (cutoff phenomenon) in a Markov process, where the distance to the stationary state collapses abruptly within a window much narrower than the relaxation time, and whose critical location is pinned by the front/extremal-particle velocity of a branching random walk
brief_reason: The abstract proves a total-variation cutoff whose location C_BRW*n*log(n) is set by the speed of the leftmost particle in a branching random walk, i.e. an abrupt equilibration threshold governed by a branching-random-walk front speed.
